reply:
[[BLOCK:srt_218]]
Speaker 1: It truly is a beautiful moment when students come together with their families to celebrate this hard-earned and meaningful achievement.

[[BLOCK:srt_219]]
Speaker 1: And there’s something about the vibrant atmosphere of a commencement ceremony in San Francisco that few other occasions can match.

[[BLOCK:srt_220]]
Speaker 1: We typically hold the ceremony in a baseball stadium, packed with graduates and their families.

[[BLOCK:srt_221]]
Speaker 1: Being there in person and truly feeling that you’re part of this community is an incredibly special and rare experience.

[[BLOCK:srt_222]]
Speaker 1: Many of our students are working professionals, living across the United States and around the world.

[[BLOCK:srt_223]]
Speaker 1: When everyone comes together to celebrate their achievements and the strength of our global community, the moment is extraordinarily meaningful.

[[BLOCK:srt_224]]
Speaker 1: The commencement ceremony at Golden Gate University truly stands out.

[[BLOCK:srt_225]]
Speaker 1: I’ve worked at several universities, but when you stand there and look out at the incredible global diversity of our student body, the sense of awe and emotion is almost impossible to describe.

[[BLOCK:srt_226]]
Speaker 1: We genuinely hope our students can attend in person.

[[BLOCK:srt_227]]
Speaker 3: Do you personally present their diplomas?

[[BLOCK:srt_228]]
Speaker 1: When students attend in person, they walk across the stage, shake hands with the provost and the president, and—most importantly—are recognized and celebrated by the entire audience for this major milestone in their lives.

[[BLOCK:srt_229]]
Speaker 1: Of course, we value the chance to shake their hands and congratulate them ourselves, but what matters most is being there to share in their celebration.
